title: encephalartos relictus
intro: Encephalartos relictus, also known as the Knysna Cycad, is a rare and endangered species of cycad, a type of ancient plant that is often referred to as a "living fossil." The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Encephalartos relictus are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
